author: yv-was-taken
title: Silent Suppression Loss on Out-of-Scope Auto-Resolution
notes: Code does clear suppression fields in _mark_auto_resolved(), but the claimed data loss scenario is wrong. Suppression is always recomputed from ignore patterns on every upsert_issues() call. When the issue comes back into scope, upsert_issues checks matched_ignore_pattern() and re-suppresses if the pattern still exists. While auto-resolved, suppression state is irrelevant (issue is not shown as open). The clearing is cosmetically unnecessary but harmless — not a real data loss bug.
